subtitle: When Tamil Became an Ayodhya of Words

panels.shot: low-angle medium shot of Kambar standing beneath a temple gopuram, palm-leaf manuscript in hand, river and village fields behind him
panels.action: Kambar stands poised with a palm-leaf bundle in his hand, his face calm yet intense, as a Chola-era temple tower rises behind him and the Cauvery-side countryside stretches into the hazy distance.

dialogue.text: In the late Chola age, as stone towers rose to the skies, Kambar forged another monument—Ayodhya itself, reborn in Tamil song.

i18n.hi: चोलों के आख़िरी दौर में, जब पत्थर के गोपुर आकाश छू रहे थे, कंबर ने एक और स्मारक गढ़ा—तमिल गीतों में जन्मी नई अयोध्या।

panels.shot: broad aerial view over the Cauvery delta, showing Therazhundur village, shining paddy fields and temple spires in the distance
panels.action: An expansive view of Therazhundur shows clustered tiled roofs, lush paddy fields fed by a shimmering canal of the Cauvery, and the silhouettes of Chola temples rising beyond the mist.

dialogue.text: In the green belly of the Cauvery delta, where canals glitter and fields breathe, stood Therazhundur—a small village in a vast Chola world.

i18n.hi: कावेरी डेल्टा की हरियाली में, जहाँ नहरें चमकतीं, खेत सांस लेते, वहीं था थेराजुंदूर—विशाल चोल साम्राज्य में एक छोटा सा गांव।

panels.shot: warm interior medium shot of a simple village house, young Kambar seated cross-legged by a lamp, listening to an elder’s unseen voice
panels.action: A youthful Kambar sits cross-legged in a modest mud-walled room, eyes wide in the lamplight, as the shadowed hands of an elder gesture while reciting from a palm leaf just beyond the frame.

dialogue.text: Here, a boy sat by flickering oil lamps and drank in stories—of Rama, of kings and forests—told in Sanskrit, in Tamil, in the murmured tongue of his elders.

i18n.hi: यहीं एक बालक टिमटिमाते दीयों तले बैठकर कहानियाँ पीता—राम की, राजाओं और वन की—संस्कृत में, तमिल में, और बूढ़ों की धीमी बोली में।

panels.shot: over-the-shoulder close shot of Kambar’s hands tracing letters on sand, with a temple school in the background
panels.action: Kambar kneels in a temple courtyard, carefully tracing letters and simple verses in the sand with his finger while other students chant near a stone-pillared hall behind him.

dialogue.text: The Chola schools taught him grammar and metre, the tight rules of verse; but his heart ran ahead of the lesson, hearing rhythm in every birdcall, in every temple bell.

i18n.hi: चोल गुरुकुलों ने उसे व्याकरण, छंद, कड़ी कड़ी नियम सिखाए; पर उसका मन पाठ से आगे दौड़ता, हर पंछी की टेर, हर घंटी में छंद सुनता।

panels.shot: wide shot in a Chola temple courtyard, Kambar reciting to a small gathering of villagers, towering gopuram and inscriptions visible
panels.action: In the shadow of a tall gopuram engraved with Chola inscriptions, a slightly older Kambar stands barefoot, reciting verses to a circle of villagers seated on the stone floor, their faces lit with quiet wonder.

dialogue.text: As Chola kings carved their names into stone, Kambar carved his into sound, reciting new Tamil verses that blended village speech with the polished music of the court.

i18n.hi: जब चोल राजा अपना नाम पत्थर पर तराश रहे थे, कंबर ने अपना नाम ध्वनि में उकेरा, नई तमिल पंक्तियाँ रचकर—गाँव की बोली को दरबारी संगीत से मिलाकर।

panels.shot: interior wide shot of a Chola royal hall, low-angle view of courtiers and scholars seated as Kambar stands at one side with a manuscript
panels.action: Inside a richly carved audience hall with lotus pillars and muraled walls, rows of Brahmin scholars and court officials sit in orderly lines while Kambar, a little apart, clutches his palm leaves and watches, half eager, half unsure.

dialogue.text: The imperial court at Gangaikonda Cholapuram thirsted for song—praising gods, kings, conquests. Poets came with garlands of words. In time, so did Kambar.

i18n.hi: गंगैकोंड चोलपुरम का शाही दरबार गीतों को तरसता था—देवों, राजाओं, विजयों की स्तुति के लिए। कवि शब्दों की मालाएँ लेकर आते। कुछ समय बाद, कंबर भी पहुँचे।

panels.shot: medium shot of a Chola patron-noble listening, while Kambar recites from a palm-leaf, backdrop of Ramayana sculptures on temple walls
panels.action: A dignified Chola nobleman, adorned with jewelry and a fine cotton shawl, sits on a low platform as Kambar stands before him, reciting with raised palm-leaf in hand; behind them, carved panels show scenes from Rama’s life.

dialogue.text: To these patrons, Rama was already known through Valmiki’s tongue and temple sculpture. Yet in their own courts, the story still wore a northern voice.

i18n.hi: इन संरक्षकों के लिए राम पहले से ही वाल्मीकि की जिह्वा और मंदिरों की मूर्तियों से परिचित थे। पर उनके दरबारों में भी यह कथा अब तक उत्तरी आवाज़ में बोलती थी।

panels.shot: tight close-up on Kambar’s face, eyes lit with resolve, as if an idea has just struck him
panels.action: Kambar’s face fills the frame, brows furrowed and eyes bright, as if some inner vision has taken hold; the blurred hint of a Ramayana sculpture can be seen behind him.

dialogue.text: Kambar felt a lack—a gap between the Rama of distant Sanskrit and the Rama of his mother’s kitchen, his village’s riverbank, his own tongue.

i18n.hi: कंबर को एक कमी चुभी—दूर की संस्कृत वाली रामकथा और उसकी माँ की रसोई, उसके गाँव के घाट, उसकी अपनी ज़बान वाले राम के बीच एक फ़ासला।

panels.shot: evening interior shot, Kambar alone by lamplight, hunched over palm-leaves, with ink-pot and stylus, stacks of leaves around
panels.action: By a dim oil lamp, Kambar bends low over a spread of palm leaves, stylus in hand, surrounded by neat stacks of completed bundles, his shadow large on the wall, the night outside pressing in through a small window.

dialogue.text: Night after night, he scratched lines into leaf and memory, not copying Valmiki word for word, but pouring Rama’s journey into the metres, idioms and images of Tamil.

i18n.hi: रात दर रात वह पत्तों और स्मृति पर पंक्तियाँ उकेरता, वाल्मीकि की नकल नहीं, बल्कि राम की यात्रा को तमिल के छंद, कहावतों और चित्रों में उड़ेलता।

panels.shot: medium shot of Kambar pausing mid-writing, looking out through a window at farmers and river, as if drawing from them
panels.action: Kambar lifts his head from writing to gaze out a small lattice window, where farmers walk along the bund of a canal and women carry pots to the river, the life of the village unfolding under a soft sky.

dialogue.text: He set Ayodhya by the Cauvery’s banks, gave forest paths the smell of his own soil, and tied the divine to the daily—so a villager could see Rama in the neighbour next door.

i18n.hi: उसने अयोध्या को कावेरी किनारे बसा दिया, जंगल की पगडंडियों में अपनी मिट्टी की गंध भरी, देव को रोज़मर्रा से जोड़ा—ताकि गाँव वाला पड़ोसी में भी राम देख सके।

panels.shot: wide temple hall shot with Kambar standing near a sanctum doorway, preparing to recite, packed crowd of devotees seated
panels.action: In a packed temple hall lit by rows of lamps, devotees sit shoulder to shoulder as Kambar stands near the doorway of the sanctum, holding his bound palm-leaf manuscript close to his chest before beginning.

dialogue.text: When at last his Ramavataram took shape, he did not unveil it in some far court first, but in front of pillars and gods—before the people whose tongue it carried.

i18n.hi: जब अंत में उसका रामावतारम आकार में ढला, उसने उसे किसी दूर दरबार में नहीं, बल्कि स्तंभों और देवमूर्तियों के सामने सुनाया—उन्हीं लोगों के बीच, जिनकी ज़बान वह ढो रहा था।

panels.shot: profile medium shot of Kambar reciting with passion, devotees in the blurred foreground visibly moved
panels.action: In profile, Kambar recites with steady passion, his hand lifted as if painting the air, while in the blurred foreground an old woman wipes a tear and a young man leans forward, captivated.

dialogue.text: As he sang of Rama’s exile and Sita’s trial, the hall fell into a listening silence so deep that the smallest sob, the faintest sigh, seemed like part of the metre.

i18n.hi: जब वह राम के वनवास और सीता की अग्नि-परीक्षा गा रहा था, सभा पर ऐसी सुनती चुप छा गई कि सबसे हल्की सिसकी, सबसे धीमी आह भी मानो छंद का हिस्सा लगे।

panels.shot: medium shot of a Chola scholar and a temple priest conferring while Kambar stands at a respectful distance
panels.action: A learned Chola scholar and a temple priest sit together under a carved arch, whispering as they consult a palm-leaf, while Kambar waits a few steps away, hands joined, his face calm but searching.

dialogue.text: Scholars weighed his work—its fidelity to Valmiki, its theology, its taste—seeing in it not mere devotion, but a new weaving of old threads into a Tamil cloth.

i18n.hi: पंडितों ने उसकी रचना तौली—वाल्मीकि से नज़दीकी, उसका दर्शन, उसका रस—और उसमें केवल भक्ति नहीं, पुराने धागों से बुना एक नया तमिल वस्त्र देखा।

panels.shot: low-angle shot in a modest court or council hall, noble patron gesturing approvingly as Kambar bows
panels.action: In a modest council hall with painted beams, a Chola nobleman seated on a low throne extends his hand in approval while Kambar bows deeply before him, holding his manuscript at his heart.

dialogue.text: “Let this Rama be sung wherever Tamil is spoken.”

i18n.hi: "यह राम वहीं गूँजे, जहाँ भी तमिल बोली जाए।"

panels.shot: montage-style wide shot: travelling bard reciting in a village square, another in a riverbank festival, with Kambar’s figure faintly superimposed
panels.action: A composite scene shows one bard reciting from Kambar’s verses in a village square, another chanting by a riverbank festival, while a faint, almost ghostly image of Kambar watches over them from the sky like a memory.

dialogue.text: Soon, his Ramayana walked without him—on the lips of bards, in the courtyards of farmers, in the great festivals where gods were carried through floodlit streets.

i18n.hi: जल्द ही उसकी रामायण उससे आगे निकल चली—गायकों की ज़ुबान पर, किसानों की चौपालों में, उन भव्य उत्सवों में जहाँ देवता रोशनी से नहाई गलियों में उठाए जाते।

panels.shot: somber wide shot of a fading Chola temple town, some structures in disrepair, with Kambar older, walking quietly through
panels.action: An older Kambar walks slowly through a once-grand Chola temple town where some walls are cracked and weeds creep between stones, while merchants still trade in the street and a lone sculptor chips away at a pillar.

dialogue.text: Around him, the Chola glory dimmed—wars at the borders, coffers thinning, stone cities losing their fresh shine. Empires, he saw, age like men.

i18n.hi: उसके चारों ओर चोल वैभव मद्धम पड़ने लगा—सीमाओं पर युद्ध, खाली होता खज़ाना, नई-नई नगरीयों की फीकी पड़ती चकाचौंध। उसने देखा, साम्राज्य भी इंसानों की तरह बूढ़े होते हैं।

panels.shot: intimate close shot of Kambar seated quietly, handing his manuscript to a younger disciple in a simple house
panels.action: Inside a simple house, Kambar sits on a reed mat and gently passes his bundled palm-leaf manuscript into the waiting hands of a younger disciple, their faces lit by soft daylight from a doorway.

dialogue.text: “Guard these words. Let them live where stones may fall.”

i18n.hi: "इन शब्दों की रखवाली करो। पत्थर गिर भी जाएँ, इन्हें जीने दो।"

panels.shot: wide temporal crossfade: ruins of old Chola structures merging into a modern Tamil town with a temple where people still listen to a Kamba Ramayanam recital
panels.action: A crumbling Chola mandapa dissolves into a modern Tamil town street with buses and shops, yet the same temple stands at the centre, where a crowd gathers inside to listen to a seated reciter with palm leaves.

dialogue.text: Centuries turned. Chola banners fell, capitals shifted, new powers rose. But in temples and homes, Kambar’s Tamil Rama still walked, his voice the bridge from kingless past to changing present.

i18n.hi: सदियाँ बदलीं। चोल ध्वज गिरे, राजधानियाँ खिसकीं, नई ताकतें उठीं। पर मंदिरों और घरों में कंबर का तमिल राम अब भी चलता रहा, उसकी आवाज़ राजविहीन अतीत से बदलते वर्तमान तक का सेतु बनी।

panels.shot: symbolic high-angle shot of an enormous open palm-leaf manuscript, its lines forming a path, with a small, iconic figure of Kambar beside it
panels.action: An enormous open palm-leaf manuscript lies like a landscape, its inscribed lines stretching into the distance like a road, while a small iconic figure of Kambar stands beside it, as if inviting the viewer to walk that path.

dialogue.text: So Kambar proved that an empire’s mightiest monument may be unseen—etched not in granite, but in language—and that long after thrones crumble, a people endure in the stories they dare to call their own.

i18n.hi: इस तरह कंबर ने दिखाया कि साम्राज्य का सबसे महान स्मारक अक्सर अदृश्य होता है—पत्थर में नहीं, भाषा में उकेरा हुआ—और कि सिंहासन ढह जाने के बाद भी लोग उन कथाओं में ज़िंदा रहते हैं, जिन्हें वे अपना कहने की हिम्मत करते हैं।

subtitle_i18n.en: When Tamil Became an Ayodhya of Words
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब तमिल बनी शब्दों की अयोध्या
